touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Lauren Garcia at SummitNetworks introducing AI-powered conversation intelligence for sales teams. Rep highlighted rep productivity gains and automation capabilities. Lauren responded expressing interest in exploring alternatives to their current evaluation of Fathom.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call to discuss current workflow and pain points

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification call with Lauren Garcia. Confirmed SummitNetworks is evaluating conversation intelligence tools to improve sales rep productivity across 66-person sales team. Lauren mentioned they're currently looking at Fathom but open to alternatives. Rep qualified budget authority and timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for conversation intelligence right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our reps are spending too much time on manual note-taking and CRM updates. We need better call analysis and automation to help them focus on selling.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We're hoping to have something in place within the next 6-8 weeks. Lauren has budget authority for tools under $100k.

next_steps: Lauren to share more details on current workflow via email
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from rep recapping qualification call and sharing relevant case study on rep productivity improvements. Lauren responded confirming interest and providing additional context on their current manual processes and integration requirements with Salesforce.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate natively with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have a native Salesforce integration that automatically syncs call summaries, action items, and custom fields.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for following Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent additional resources on automation capabilities and consulting services ahead of discovery call. Lauren acknowledged receipt and confirmed the discovery call time. Brief exchange, no substantive discussion.
next_steps: Discovery call confirmed for Tuesday 2pm ET

touches.ai_summary: In-depth discovery call with Lauren Garcia covering current state challenges, use cases, and success metrics. Lauren detailed their manual call note-taking process, CRM data quality issues, and onboarding challenges. Rep walked through automation capabilities and consulting offering. Lauren mentioned Fathom is cheaper but lacks the consulting support they need for adoption.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through what happens after a sales call today - how do reps capture and log information?
questions_asked.answer: Reps manually type notes into Salesforce, which takes 10-15 minutes per call. Data quality is inconsistent and managers don't have good visibility into deal risks.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: Reduce time spent on admin work by 30%, improve CRM data completeness to above 80%, and give managers better coaching insights.

questions_asked.question: How does your consulting engagement work - is that an additional cost?
questions_asked.answer: Consulting is included in our automations_plus_consulting package. We provide onboarding, custom workflow setup, and ongoing optimization support.

objections: Fathom is significantly cheaper at around $60k for our seat count. We need to justify the price difference.
next_steps: Rep to prepare ROI analysis comparing automation + consulting value
Lauren to introduce Fatima Richter (IT Director) for technical evaluation
Schedule technical call for following week

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Fatima Richter (IT Director) covering security, compliance, data handling, and integration architecture. Fatima asked detailed questions about SOC 2, data residency, API rate limits, and Salesforce field mapping. Rep walked through security documentation and integration capabilities. Fatima expressed satisfaction with technical requirements but flagged need for IT change management support.

questions_asked.question: What's your SOC 2 compliance status and where is data stored?
questions_asked.answer: We're SOC 2 Type II certified and offer US-based data residency. All conversation data is encrypted at rest and in transit.

questions_asked.question: How does the Salesforce integration handle custom fields and object mapping?
questions_asked.answer: Our integration supports custom object and field mapping through a configuration UI. We can map AI-extracted data to any custom Salesforce field.

questions_asked.question: What kind of IT support do you provide during rollout?
questions_asked.answer: Our consulting team includes IT onboarding support - we'll work with your team on SSO setup, integration configuration, and user provisioning.

questions_asked.question: Are there any technical blockers or concerns from your side?
questions_asked.answer: No major blockers. Main concern is change management and user adoption, which is why the consulting piece is valuable.

next_steps: Fatima to review security documentation and share with InfoSec team
Rep to send technical integration guide
Move forward with demo for Lauren and sales leadership

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from rep to Lauren recapping discovery call discussion and attaching ROI analysis showing projected $180k annual value from time savings and improved win rates. Lauren responded positively and confirmed she's briefed Brandon Lim (CRO) on the evaluation.
next_steps: Demo scheduled for next week with Lauren and sales managers

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent demo preparation email with agenda and use cases to cover. Lauren confirmed attendance and requested focus on automation workflows and manager coaching features. Brief logistical exchange.

questions_asked.question: Can you show examples of the automated CRM field population in the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - we'll walk through live examples of how conversation data automatically populates opportunity fields, contact roles, and next steps.

next_steps: Demo confirmed for Thursday 3pm ET

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Lauren Garcia and sales managers. Rep demonstrated call recording, AI summarization, automated Salesforce sync, and manager coaching dashboard. Lauren and team responded positively to automation capabilities and asked detailed questions about custom workflow setup. Team particularly interested in deal risk identification features. Note: This meeting was rescheduled once due to scheduling conflict.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize what fields get auto-populated in Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, field mapping is fully customizable through the admin console. You can map any AI-extracted data point to Salesforce fields.

questions_asked.question: How does the deal risk scoring work?
questions_asked.answer: Our AI analyzes conversation patterns, stakeholder engagement, objections, and timeline mentions to surface potential risks. Managers get alerts on at-risk deals.

questions_asked.question: What questions do you have about implementing this with your team?
questions_asked.answer: Main question is how long the rollout takes and what the learning curve looks like for reps.

next_steps: Lauren to get budget approval from Brandon Lim (CRO)
Rep to prepare formal proposal with pricing and implementation timeline
Schedule proposal review call

touches.ai_summary: Rep sent formal proposal email with pricing breakdown: $97,200 ACV for 66 seats with automations_plus_consulting package, including 8-week implementation and ongoing support. Lauren acknowledged receipt and scheduled proposal review call. Mentioned she's working to get Brandon Lim on the call.
next_steps: Proposal review call scheduled
Lauren working to include Brandon Lim (CRO)

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Lauren Garcia. Rep walked through pricing structure, implementation timeline, and ROI projections. Lauren confirmed budget is approved in principle but needs Brandon Lim's sign-off on final numbers. Discussed potential start date and team rollout phasing.

questions_asked.question: Can we phase the rollout - start with 30 seats and expand?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss phasing, but pricing is most competitive at the full 66-seat commitment. We could do a pilot group for initial onboarding.

questions_asked.question: What's Brandon's main concern or priority for this purchase?
questions_asked.answer: He wants to make sure we're getting better value than Fathom and that the consulting support will actually drive adoption. He's been burned by tools that don't get used.

next_steps: Schedule executive meeting with Brandon Lim
Rep to prepare executive-level value summary

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Brandon Lim (CRO) and Lauren Garcia. Rep presented strategic value proposition focused on rep productivity, revenue acceleration, and competitive win rates. Brandon challenged on Fathom pricing comparison and asked about proof of consulting ROI. Rep shared customer references and success metrics. Brandon expressed support but wants to negotiate pricing before final approval.

questions_asked.question: Fathom is offering us $58k for 66 seats - how do you justify the $40k premium?
questions_asked.answer: The delta is primarily the consulting and custom automation setup. Fathom is recording and transcription - we're providing full workflow automation, change management, and ongoing optimization that drives actual adoption.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical adoption rate with the consulting package?
questions_asked.answer: With our consulting engagement, we see 85%+ active usage within 90 days vs. 40-50% for self-serve implementations.

questions_asked.question: If we can demonstrate clear ROI and support your team through implementation, are you comfortable with this investment level?
questions_asked.answer: I need to see if we can get closer to $85k to make this work. If we can land there, I'm ready to move forward.

objections: Price premium over Fathom is significant - need to get closer to their pricing or provide more justification
next_steps: Move to pricing negotiation
Rep to discuss discount options with sales leadership
Target close within 2 weeks

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call with Lauren Garcia. Rep presented revised proposal at $92,000 (5% discount) with extended payment terms. Lauren appreciated the movement but said Brandon is firm on $85k target. Discussed potential to reduce consulting scope or seat count to hit number. Call rescheduled once due to Lauren's conflict. Productive but no resolution yet.

questions_asked.question: If we reduce to 60 seats, what would pricing look like?
questions_asked.answer: At 60 seats we'd be around $88,200. Still above your $85k target.

questions_asked.question: Is the $85k a hard ceiling or is there flexibility if we can show incremental value?
questions_asked.answer: Brandon has been pretty firm on that number. He's comparing directly to the Fathom quote.

objections: Still need to get to $85k to get Brandon's approval - current offer not quite there
next_steps: Rep to explore creative pricing structures
Reconvene in 2 days to discuss options
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up pricing call with Lauren (rescheduled once from previous day). Rep proposed alternative: $85,800 annual price with quarterly payments and reduced consulting to 6-week implementation instead of 8-week. Lauren receptive but wants to confirm with Brandon that reduced implementation timeline is acceptable. Conversation focused on trade-offs.

questions_asked.question: What's at risk with a 6-week vs 8-week implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Main difference is pacing - we'd compress onboarding and workflow setup. Still fully supported, just faster cadence.

questions_asked.question: If we can land at $85,800 with the 6-week timeline, does that work for Brandon?
questions_asked.answer: I think that gets us very close. Let me confirm he's okay with the faster implementation and get back to you.

next_steps: Lauren to confirm with Brandon
Rep to formalize revised proposal at $85,800

touches.ai_summary: Brief check-in call with Lauren. She confirmed Brandon is comfortable with $85,800 and 6-week implementation. Discussed final contract details and procurement process. Lauren introduced Chris Ahmed from procurement to handle contracting and mentioned Fatima will need to review MSA terms.
next_steps: Rep to send final contract to Chris Ahmed (procurement)
Lauren to coordinate internal approvals
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Final alignment call with Lauren Garcia. Confirmed contract sent to Chris Ahmed and legal review in progress. Discussed implementation kickoff timing and team communication plan. Lauren confirmed all stakeholders aligned and expecting signature within 48 hours. Rep outlined post-signature onboarding steps.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation once contract is signed?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off within 5 business days of signature. Our consulting team will reach out to schedule the kickoff call.

next_steps: Contract signature expected within 48 hours
Implementation kickoff to follow
Rep to introduce customer success team
